title: oxytropis campestris subsp. jordalii
intro: Oxytropis campestris subsp. jordalii is a type of plant commonly known as a mountain milkvetch, which is a member of the legume family and native to Norway. The plant has been found to have medicinal properties, particularly as a natural source of glycosides, flavonoids, and alkaloids that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Oxytropis campestris subsp. jordalii include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and act as an antimicrobial agent. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential uses.
